refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional requirement: supporting teleoperation with a specified communication quality metric (packet loss limit). It does not combine multiple obligations and avoids specifying how the communication quality is achieved, thus remaining solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions
Incorporate error detection and correction mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication quality monitoring and feedback system to ensure packet loss targets are met
Use redundant communication channels to mitigate packet loss and ensure reliability
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical approach to maintain communication quality independently by adapting to network conditions, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be accurately monitored and parameters adjusted in real-time
Risk that adaptive protocol complexity may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes that packet loss can be effectively controlled through protocol adaptation alone
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and feedback mechanisms as separate DPs or sub-functions to ensure clear separation of concerns and support modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network and communication management including adaptive protocols

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving data over the network, essential for adaptive communication.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides data output from the communication module to other system parts.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol is core to network management; minimal diffusion since protocol logic is centralized here.
Vehicle module uses communication but implementing adaptive protocol here would cause diffusion into network management concerns.
Teleoperation depends on communication but adaptive protocol is not its core function, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, not communication protocols, so containment is weak and diffusion high.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the adaptive communication protocol DP, as it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate protocol adaptation logic with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time network condition data from other modules may cause some interface complexity.
If network link interface changes, adaptive protocol implementation may require updates.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions' directly addresses the FR requirement to support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit. The adaptive protocol logically fulfills the need to maintain packet loss ≤ 1% under varying network conditions, satisfying the acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time network condition data from other modules may cause some interface complexity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is appropriately chosen as the IM boundary, with high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating that the adaptive communication protocol DP is well centralized within this module. Other modules show poor containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the decision to localize the DP within NGHE Solution to minimize diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics over the Network Link interface under agreed network conditions, ensuring the adaptive protocol meets the ≤1% packet loss acceptance criterion.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the adaptive communication protocol DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and unambiguous, with well-defined interfaces (Network Link and Telemetry) supporting the implementation and verification of the FR.

recommended_revisions: Consider defining explicit interface contracts for real-time network condition data to reduce complexity and potential coupling.
Document contingency plans for handling changes in the Network Link interface to minimize impact on the adaptive protocol implementation.
Include verification procedures in the NGHE Solution module to continuously monitor packet loss and adapt transmission parameters accordingly.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification that the DP fully satisfies the FR, the IM boundary is well chosen with minimal diffusion risk, and verification feasibility is confirmed. The recommended revisions are minor improvements related to interface contracts and verification procedures, not fundamental decomposition needs. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed and stable to proceed without further sub-FR derivation at this time.

revise.why: Incorporate recommended revisions to define explicit interface contracts for real-time network data, document contingency plans for interface changes, and include verification procedures within the NGHE Solution module to ensure continuous compliance with packet loss requirements.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol DP fully addresses the FR under all expected network conditions.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate architectural boundary for implementing the DP.
Verification through packet loss monitoring is sufficient to confirm FR satisfaction.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity due to dependency on real-time network condition data.
Changes in Network Link interface may impact adaptive protocol implementation if not properly managed.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss ≤1% under varying network conditions during teleoperation.
Documentation of interface contracts and contingency plans.
Verification procedures embedded in NGHE Solution module.
